response.justification: The candidate is an experienced full-stack software developer with strong web, testing, and craftsmanship credentials, but the profile is fundamentally misaligned with a Principal Performance Architect role focused on SoC/IP modeling and AI accelerator optimization. There is no evidence of hardware architecture, GPU/SIMD experience, deep-learning workload analysis, or hardware-software co-design. The closest relevant experience — low-latency DLang services at Dunnhumby — is systems-adjacent but far from silicon-level performance work.
strengths: Solid low-latency systems experience (async I/O, fibers, caching) in high-throughput environments
Long, consistent engineering career with open-source contributions and community teaching
Strong software craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, testable design)
concerns: No SoC, IP modeling, GPU architecture, or hardware performance experience
Career trajectory is web/product engineering, not principal-level hardware performance architecture
